01_initial_request.input_data: Simulated and real multi-workflow instances, heterogeneous cloud resource configurations (VMs and containers), and dynamic electricity price data
01_initial_request.user_intent: To economically schedule multi-workflows on cloud platforms by minimizing total electricity cost while satisfying workflow deadline constraints
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Minimize Total Electricity Cost (TEC) achieving 54.5–69.1% average savings compared to existing approaches while satisfying deadline constraints

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multi-workflows are deployed on cloud platforms with heterogeneous resources and dynamic electricity prices. [Gap] Existing studies focus on single-workflows or constant electricity prices, ignoring hybrid tasks and dynamic costs. [Decision] To achieve minimal TEC while satisfying deadlines, chose to use Mathematical Modeling to perform problem formulation on multi-workflow scheduling, expecting to obtain a formal optimization model with constraints.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Defined the multi-workflow scheduling problem with TEC minimization objective and three constraints.

references.citation: Feller E, Rilling L, Morin C (2011) Energy-aware ant colony based workload placement in clouds
references.relevance: Provides the power-utilization model used for electricity cost calculation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Different workflow scheduling sequences generate different TECs. [Gap] Need a rule to sort workflows based on characteristics like deadline and workload. [Decision] To achieve optimal WSS, chose to use WSSG component to perform workflow priority assignment on multi-workflow instances, expecting to obtain a sorted workflow list.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated Workflow Scheduling Sequence (WSS) based on descending order of priorities determined by WDF rule.

references.citation: Demeulemeester EL, Herroelen WS (2006) Project Scheduling: A Research Handbook
references.relevance: Provides critical-path based dynamic programming method for estimating earliest finish time.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Task precedences imply data waiting time affecting TEC. [Gap] Need an initial task sequence considering sub-deadlines. [Decision] To achieve efficient search, chose to use TSSI component with DUSD algorithm to perform task priority assignment on each workflow, expecting to obtain an initial task scheduling sequence.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated initial Task Scheduling Sequence (TSS) for each workflow using SSTF rule and updated sub-deadlines.

references.citation: Xie G, Jiang J, Liu Y, Li R, Li K (2017) Minimizing energy consumption of real-time parallel applications
references.relevance: Referenced for sub-deadline distribution based on depth and floating time.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Each task needs a server and time block to minimize cost. [Gap] Global search is computationally expensive. [Decision] To enhance search efficiency, chose to use OSSD component (ACSS and OSSS) to perform candidate server search and scheme determination on each task, expecting to obtain the optimal scheduling scheme for each task.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Determined Optimal Scheduling Scheme (OSS) for each task specifying server, CPU cores, and time block with minimal electricity cost.

references.citation: Topcuoglu H, Hariri S, Wu M-Y (2002) Performance-effective and low-complexity task scheduling for heterogeneous computing
references.relevance: Referenced for approximating average execution time.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial TSS may be a local optimal solution. [Gap] Need to explore better sequences to reduce TEC further. [Decision] To avoid local optimization, chose to use TSSO component with VND algorithm to perform task-pair-swap actions on the initial TSS, expecting to obtain an optimized task scheduling sequence.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Optimized the initial TSS to generate a superior sequence with lower TEC.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] TSSO satisfies constraints but may leave servers under-utilized. [Gap] Inefficient servers increase idle power cost. [Decision] To improve resource utilization, chose to use OSPO component with container migration to perform server rescheduling on inefficient servers, expecting to obtain the final optimal scheduling plan.

parameters.time_complexity: O(max{M log M, M sum n_w})

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated final Optimal Scheduling Plan (OSP) by migrating containers from inefficient servers.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] HEMS has five components with multiple rules and parameters. [Gap] Best combination of rules and parameters is unknown. [Decision] To configure HEMS optimally, chose to use ANOVA technique to perform multi-factor analysis on RPD, expecting to obtain the best parameter settings.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Calibrated best parameters: WDF, SSTF, MAECF, gamma=0.5, phi=25%.

references.citation: Bartz-Beielstein T, Chiarandini M, Paquete L, Preuss M (2013) Experimental methods for the analysis of optimization algorithms
references.relevance: Provides the ANOVA technique for parameter calibration.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] HEMS needs validation against existing approaches. [Gap] Performance on various multi-workflow types is unknown. [Decision] To validate superiority, chose to use WorkflowSim platform to perform simulations on 6000 instances comparing HEMS with HEFT, TUMS-WTM, DQWS, expecting to obtain TEC savings and CPU time metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: HEMS achieved 54.5–69.1% average TEC savings compared to existing approaches within slightly longer CPU time.

references.citation: Chen W, Deelman E (2012) Workflowsim: A toolkit for simulating scientific workflows in distributed environments
references.relevance: Provides the simulation platform used for performance evaluation.

references.citation: Topcuoglu H, Hariri S, Wu M-Y (2002) Performance-effective and low-complexity task scheduling for heterogeneous computing
references.relevance: Describes the HEFT algorithm used for comparison.

references.citation: Zhang C, Wang Y, Feng Z, Guo H (2017) Power consumption optimization for deadline-constrained workflows in cloud data center
references.relevance: Describes the TUMS-WTM algorithm used for comparison.

references.citation: Khojasteh Toussi G, Naghibzadeh M (2021) A divide and conquer approach to deadline constrained cost-optimization workflow scheduling for the cloud
references.relevance: Describes the DQWS algorithm used for comparison.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Simulation on WorkflowSim with ANOVA calibration and comparative analysis

TEC_Savings.interpretation: Average savings compared to HEFT, DQWS, and TUMS-WTM respectively.

RPD_Reduction.interpretation: RPD reductions compared to HEFT, DQWS, and TUMS-WTM respectively.

CPU_Time.interpretation: HEMS requires slightly longer CPU time but achieves optimal scheduling plan.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. HEMS consistently minimizes Total Electricity Cost while satisfying deadline constraints, outperforming existing approaches.
